subject: Chemistry
title: The Island of Stability
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Island of Stability usable.

hook.text: Physicists believe that somewhere beyond the current table, superheavy elements might be surprisingly stable.

buildup.text: Most superheavy elements decay in milliseconds. They're so unstable they barely count as existing.

discovery.text: Theory predicts 'magic numbers' of protons and neutrons that make nuclei extra stable—like closed shells.

twist.text: The island might center around element 114 or 120. Some could last minutes, hours, or even years.

climax.text: If stable superheavy elements exist, they could have unique properties unlike anything we've seen.

punchline.text: An island of stability in a sea of decay. The table's hidden treasure.

quiz.question: What is the 'island of stability'?
options: A predicted region where superheavy elements might be surprisingly long-lived
An island where rare elements are mined
